title: echinocereus dasyacanthus subsp. rectispinus
intro: Echinocereus dasyacanthus subsp. rectispinus, a species of cactus, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the Cactaceae family, commonly found in the southwestern United States and Mexico. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Echinocereus dasyacanthus subsp. rectispinus include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and support immune function. Herbal preparations of this plant include infusions, decoctions, and salves, which can be used topically or internally, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
